checklist_item: Verify current I-9 form version is in use
notes: Check USCIS website for current version. Using an expired form is a violation.

checklist_item: Section 1 completed by employee on or before first day of work
notes: Employee must complete Section 1 no later than the end of Day 1. Do not complete before offer is accepted.

checklist_item: Section 2 completed by employer within 3 business days of hire
notes: Examine original documents in person. Photocopies not acceptable as originals.

checklist_item: Acceptable documents reviewed from List A or Lists B+C
notes: Cannot specify which documents an employee must present. Accept any valid combination from the I-9 lists.

checklist_item: Documents appear reasonably genuine and relate to the employee
notes: Employer is not required to be a document expert. Standard is 'reasonable person' review.

checklist_item: No over-documentation (requesting more documents than required)
notes: Requesting additional or specific documents beyond what the employee chooses is an unfair immigration practice.

checklist_item: Reverification completed for expiring work authorization
notes: Set calendar reminders 90 days before expiration. Use Section 3 or new I-9. Do not reverify US citizens or permanent residents.
A receipt for a lost/stolen document is valid for 90 days. Employee must present the actual document before the 90-day period ends.

checklist_item: I-9 forms stored separately from personnel files
notes: I-9s must be kept in a separate file or binder. Makes audit response easier and protects employee privacy.

checklist_item: Retention schedule followed — 3 years from hire or 1 year from termination (whichever later)
notes: Destroy I-9s on schedule. Keeping them too long increases audit exposure. Calculate both dates and use the later one.

checklist_item: E-Verify case created within 3 business days of hire (if enrolled)
notes: Only applies to E-Verify participating employers. Federal contractors may be required to enroll. NC requires E-Verify for employers with 25+ employees.
Review all current I-9s for completeness. Correct errors using established correction procedures (single line through error, initial, date).

checklist_item: Corrections made properly (single line strikethrough with initials and date)
notes: Do not use white-out or completely obscure the original entry. The original must remain visible.

checklist_item: Missing I-9s identified and new forms completed if employee still active
notes: If an I-9 cannot be located for a current employee, have them complete a new one immediately. Note 'replacing missing I-9' in the additional information field.

checklist_item: Designated authorized representative trained on I-9 procedures
notes: Anyone completing Section 2 on behalf of the employer must understand acceptable documents and anti-discrimination rules.
